deployment_question: How does the model predict responses to perturbations absent from training?
definition: Hold out all observations for selected genetic, chemical, or combinatorial perturbations while training on other perturbations.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: sets same-perturbation train overlap to zero; related perturbations may remain nearby

implementation_recipe: Choose perturbations or perturbation classes to hold out.
Assign all cells or profiles under held-out perturbations to test.
Measure similarity from held-out perturbations to training perturbations.
quality_gates: Report whether related genes, drugs, or pathways remain in training.
Separate unseen single perturbations from unseen combinations.
Evaluate response magnitude and direction, not only aggregate expression error.
failure_modes: Unseen perturbations can still be near duplicates through pathway or chemical similarity.
Cell-state composition can differ between perturbations.
Metrics can reward predicting systematic variation rather than perturbation-specific effects.

citations.title: PerturBench: Benchmarking Machine Learning Models for Cellular Perturbation Analysis
citations.evidence: Benchmarks perturbation-response models with tasks reflecting deployment where perturbation effects are unobserved.

citations.title: PerturbNet predicts single-cell responses to unseen chemical and genetic perturbations
citations.evidence: Targets prediction for novel molecules and gene edits.
